steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the text. The passage gives two reasons scholars value juvenilia: (1) unpublished work by later-famous authors reveals their artistic development, and (2) published juvenilia by lesser-known writers is essential to understanding literary history. Both halves explain why scholars find juvenilia worth studying.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'To describe the challenges famous writers encountered when seeking to publish works written in their childhood': The passage never mentions publishing challenges. Auden's "Autumn" is described as unpublished, not as rejected or difficult to publish. ✗
  • Choice B — 'To argue that Ashford's novels have more literary merit than Auden's juvenilia do': The passage makes no comparison of merit between the two authors' works. ✗
  • Choice C — 'To compare the accomplishments of young writers with those of their adult contemporaries': The passage compares two categories of juvenilia with each other (famous vs. lesser-known), not young writers against their adult contemporaries. ✗
  • Choice D — 'To present reasons why literary scholars consider juvenilia to be valuable resources': Matches both halves of the passage: scholarly interest in famous authors' unpublished juvenilia (developmental insight) and in lesser-known writers' published juvenilia (historical insight). ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
